jokes.text: Ah, my crêpes are done.
jokes.setup: Infomercial showcasing the 'S.S. Microwave' - presumably a ship-themed microwave
jokes.punchline: Troy casually mentions making crêpes, an unexpectedly sophisticated dish for a novelty microwave
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Troy's pretentious nature even in infomercials, economical delivery

jokes.text: The Doggie Doorman - 'Good evening, Rex' - formal greeting to dog
jokes.setup: Product called 'Doggie Doorman'
jokes.punchline: Treating a dog with formal human courtesy as if it's a hotel guest
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor playing on the contrast between formal service and pet care.

jokes.text: I'm Troy McClure... star of such films as 'P' Is For Psycho... and The President's Neck Is Missing.
jokes.setup: Troy's standard introduction format
jokes.punchline: Two absurdly titled B-movies, especially 'The President's Neck Is Missing'
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure bit with perfect character integration and memorable fake movie titles

jokes.text: You mean there's a better way?
jokes.setup: Troy struggling violently to squeeze orange juice by hand
jokes.punchline: His shocked realization there might be an easier method
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Troy's exaggerated confusion

jokes.text: But won't we have to pay those outrageous grocery-store prices... for something the farmer probably spit in?
jokes.setup: Troy asking about orange juice costs
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly gross speculation about farmer contamination
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark turn in wholesome infomercial context works well

jokes.text: Doctor, are you sure it's on? I can't hear a thing. It's whisper quiet!
jokes.setup: Juice Loosener making extremely loud whirring noise
jokes.punchline: Dr. Nick claiming it's 'whisper quiet' while Troy can't hear anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction between sound and claim, well-timed

jokes.text: Sun 'n' Run... the suntan lotion that's also a laxative.
jokes.setup: Offering a bonus product with the juicer
jokes.punchline: Combining suntan lotion with laxative for no logical reason
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist product combination with clever pun name

jokes.text: Won't get chest pains from answering the phone anymore.
jokes.setup: Homer listing benefits he expects from getting healthy with juice
jokes.punchline: Revealing he gets chest pains from the minimal exertion of answering the phone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his extreme laziness/poor health revealed through casual aside

jokes.text: Please don't tell the supervisor I have the flu. I've been working with a shattered pelvis for three weeks.
jokes.setup: Barney asking Homer to keep his flu secret from work
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been working with a much more serious injury
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - flu seems minor compared to shattered pelvis

jokes.text: Homer's reaction to son's gift: 'Mother, get an extra-special hug ready'
jokes.setup: Homer excited about receiving his juicer
jokes.punchline: Overly formal announcement of affection to his wife
jokes.explanation: Characteristically awkward Homer expression of joy - formal language for simple emotion.

jokes.text: Good Lord. Flu germs entering every orifice in my head. Uhh.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Marge a juicer as a gift while sick
jokes.punchline: Marge's horrified reaction to being exposed to his germs
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Marge's germaphobia, but fairly standard

jokes.text: Route 401 is going around and around... and around and around and around.
jokes.setup: Traffic reporter giving update from helicopter
jokes.punchline: His helicopter is apparently spinning out of control
jokes.explanation: Good use of repetition to convey helicopter spinning, visual comedy

jokes.text: And look out at the corner of 12th and Main because I'm gonna be sick. Whoop.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Arnie's helicopter spinning
jokes.punchline: Warning people below before vomiting from motion sickness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the helicopter bit, economical delivery

jokes.text: Sarge, we keep getting orders to let the virus win. Must be a school day. Lay down your arms.
jokes.setup: White blood cells inside someone's body discussing battle plans
jokes.punchline: They're ordered to surrender because it's a school day (kids need to stay home)
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about kids wanting to stay home sick from school

jokes.text: All right! Let's make some pus.
jokes.setup: White blood cells surrendering to the virus
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastically embracing their new role making infection symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the medical anthropomorphization, gross-out humor

jokes.text: Hey, you, get that steel drum out of the, uh, mayor's office. Sorry, 'mon.'
jokes.setup: Mayor claiming he canceled his Bahamas vacation due to epidemic
jokes.punchline: Steel drum player in his office reveals he's actually in the Bahamas
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction between claim and reality, good visual reveal

jokes.text: Who the devil are you? Don't panic. Just come up with a good story. My name is Mr. Burns. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Burns asks Homer to identify himself in the germfree chamber
jokes.punchline: Homer's internal monologue to make up a story, then just says his boss's name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - we hear him plan to lie, then he gives the worst possible answer

jokes.text: Where do we get these placebos? Maybe there's some in this truck!
jokes.setup: Doctor explains placebos are the only cure
jokes.punchline: Crowd mishearing 'placebos' as something they can loot from a truck
jokes.explanation: Decent misunderstanding joke, shows crowd's medical ignorance

jokes.text: I'm cured. I mean, ouch!
jokes.setup: Crowd apparently found bee truck instead of medicine
jokes.punchline: Ned accidentally admits bee stings cured him before remembering to act hurt
jokes.explanation: Good quick reversal, Ned's honesty getting him in trouble

jokes.text: Dad, Todd's speaking in tongues! I wish he were, Rod. I pray for the day... but I'm afraid he's just delirious from fever.
jokes.setup: Rod thinking his brother is speaking in tongues (religious miracle)
jokes.punchline: Ned disappointed it's just fever delirium, not divine intervention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - religious fervor trumping parental concern

jokes.text: Oh, Neddy, why has God forsaken us? I can't imagine what we could have done to-- Oh.
jokes.setup: Maude wondering why God is punishing them with illness
jokes.punchline: She sees something (likely their TV) that reminds her of their sin
jokes.explanation: Good setup for religious guilt, visual punchline works with character

jokes.text: Maybe they'd feel more at home in the bedroom, Al.
jokes.setup: Al commenting that plants are lifeless and limp
jokes.punchline: Peg suggests they belong in the bedroom (implying Al is also lifeless/limp)
jokes.explanation: Standard married couple insult from Married with Children show-within-show

jokes.text: Watch Fox and be damned for all eternity.
jokes.setup: Thunder crashes during Fox programming
jokes.punchline: Ned realizes the network's slogan is literally true - watching Fox damns you
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Fox network by The Simpsons themselves, highly quotable

jokes.text: Can you get me some of those Flintstones chewable morphine?
jokes.setup: Bart asking for medicine while sick
jokes.punchline: Requesting children's vitamins format for hardcore drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist combination of children's vitamins with morphine, very quotable

jokes.text: You can stir it into my mush. Either way, just gimme, gimme, gimme!
jokes.setup: Grampa can't have liquids after 3pm but wants bourbon
jokes.punchline: Suggesting creative workaround followed by desperate pleading
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Grampa's desperation and resourcefulness

jokes.text: I lowered the price because an escaped mental patient tampered with the bottle.
jokes.setup: Marge shocked at $24.95 aspirin price
jokes.punchline: Apu casually mentions mental patient contamination as if it's a reasonable discount explanation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, perfectly captures convenience store absurdity

jokes.text: Eight foot, six.
jokes.setup: Store theft detector alarm going off
jokes.punchline: Employee measuring Marge's hair height instead of checking for theft
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag playing on Marge's distinctive hair, economical delivery

jokes.text: Apu calling out shoplifting: 'You did not pay for this bottle of Colonel Kwik-E-Mart's Kentucky Bourbon'
jokes.setup: Catching Marge shoplifting
jokes.punchline: Kwik-E-Mart branded bourbon parodying KFC
jokes.explanation: Solid brand parody mixing convenience store with bourbon branding.

jokes.text: All right, come out with your hands up... two cups of coffee... an auto freshener that says Capricorn... and something with coconut on it.
jokes.setup: Police surrounding the store for shoplifting incident
jokes.punchline: Officer making his own shopping requests during the arrest
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from serious police work to mundane shopping requests

jokes.text: By hiring me as your lawyer, you also get this smoking monkey. Better cut down there, Smokey.
jokes.setup: Hutz offering legal services to Marge
jokes.punchline: Inexplicably including a cigarette-smoking monkey as a bonus
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist humor with perfect Hutz character integration, memorable visual

jokes.text: Well, replace the word 'kinda' with the word 'repeatedly'... and the word 'dog' with 'son.'
jokes.setup: Hutz admits he 'kinda ran over the judge's dog'
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he repeatedly ran over the judge's son
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation structure, perfectly timed dark reveal, highly quotable format

jokes.text: No offense, but we're putting that bitch on ice.
jokes.setup: Apu's usually polite demeanor when Homer asks to drop charges
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly harsh language from typically courteous Apu
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion - polite Apu using surprisingly harsh language

jokes.text: Apu's corporate backing: 'Kwik-E-Mart and its parent corporation, Nordyne Defense Dynamics'
jokes.setup: Explaining prosecution policy
jokes.punchline: Convenience store owned by defense contractor
jokes.explanation: Good corporate satire - absurd connection between corner store and military contractor.

jokes.text: In other news, the chick in The Crying Game... is really a man.
jokes.setup: News anchor reporting gossip about Marge
jokes.punchline: Casually spoiling major movie twist as if it's news
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about movie spoilers, relevant to 1993 broadcast

jokes.text: Well, I'll let you in on a little secret about Marge Simpson. She's got webbed toes.
jokes.setup: Helen sharing increasingly ridiculous gossip about Marge
jokes.punchline: Claiming Marge has webbed toes as scandalous secret
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Helen's gossiping nature, absurd 'secret'

jokes.text: Are you Ed Flanders? No, Ned Flanders. My mistake.
jokes.setup: Process server looking for Ned with legal papers
jokes.punchline: Mishearing as 'Ed Flanders' then casually correcting
jokes.explanation: Simple name confusion joke, functional but not special

jokes.text: I'll never have that problem again.
jokes.setup: Process server saying he learned from the name confusion
jokes.punchline: Implication he's going to harm or eliminate Ned to avoid future confusion
jokes.explanation: Dark implication delivered casually, good economy

jokes.text: Your Honor, I feel so confident of Marge Simpson's guilt... that I can waste the court's time by rating the super hunks.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor explaining why he's asking about Tom Cruise vs Mel Gibson
jokes.punchline: Using confidence in winning as excuse for completely irrelevant behavior
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist courtroom behavior, logical within the absurd premise

jokes.text: Mrs. Lovejoy's testimony: 'And, quite frankly, I think she, um--' followed by drinky-drinky motion
jokes.setup: Testifying about Marge's character
jokes.punchline: Using gesture instead of words to imply drinking problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Helen's gossipy nature and false accusations through visual comedy.

jokes.text: Let the record show that the witness made the drinky-drinky motion.
jokes.setup: Helen Lovejoy making drinking gesture to imply Marge is an alcoholic
jokes.punchline: Court reporter's formal documentation of childish hand gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between formal court language and childish gesture, quotable

jokes.text: And if we freeze on frame 138... we distinctly see a puff of blue hair... coming from the grassy knoll.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor presenting video evidence of Marge's shoplifting
jokes.punchline: Parodying JFK assassination conspiracy theories with Marge's hair
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of conspiracy theories applied to mundane shoplifting case

jokes.text: What's that? You want me to drink you? But I'm in the middle of a trial. Excuse me.
jokes.setup: Hutz describing bourbon as evidence
jokes.punchline: Talking to the bottle as if it's requesting to be consumed, then leaving trial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hutz character moment showing his alcoholism derailing the trial

jokes.text: Hello, David. I'm really tempted. Just take it one day at a time, and know that I love you. I love you too, man.
jokes.setup: Hutz calling someone during trial break
jokes.punchline: Having AA sponsor conversation in middle of court case
jokes.explanation: Good character development for Hutz's alcoholism, realistic AA dialogue

jokes.text: No. In fact, I can recite pi to 40,000 places. The last digit is one.
jokes.setup: Apu claiming he never forgets anything
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating with mathematical impossibility (pi is infinite)
jokes.explanation: Good math joke showing Apu's intelligence while revealing mathematical error

jokes.text: Something that may shock and discredit you. And that thing is as follows. I'm not wearing a tie at all.
jokes.setup: Hutz building dramatic tension about shocking revelation
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic reveal that he's not wearing the tie Apu described
jokes.explanation: Good buildup to anticlimactic reveal, typical Hutz incompetence

jokes.text: Hutz's America comment: 'If there's one thing America needs, it's more lawyers'
jokes.setup: Bart saying he wants to be a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Stating opposite of popular opinion about lawyers
jokes.explanation: Standard anti-lawyer joke - Hutz's self-serving perspective on his profession.

jokes.text: Can you imagine a world without lawyers?
jokes.setup: Hutz suggesting more lawyers are needed
jokes.punchline: Cut to idyllic peaceful scene (implied visual), Hutz shudders in horror
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag contrasting lawyer's perspective with everyone else's

jokes.text: Mr. Hutz, do you know you're not wearing any pants?
jokes.setup: Hutz delivering closing argument
jokes.punchline: Judge noticing Hutz is pantsless during professional presentation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Hutz's incompetence to complete professional breakdown

jokes.text: I move for a bad court thingy. You mean a mistrial? Yeah. That's why you're the judge, and I'm the law-talkin' guy. The lawyer. Right.
jokes.setup: Hutz trying to use legal terminology
jokes.punchline: Completely butchering basic legal language multiple times
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hutz incompetence, 'law-talkin' guy' is highly quotable

jokes.text: This verdict is written on a cocktail napkin! And it still says 'guilty.' And 'guilty' is spelled wrong!
jokes.setup: Jury delivering verdict
jokes.punchline: Multiple layers of incompetence in the jury's verdict
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - each revealed problem gets worse

jokes.text: Don't worry, Mom. I'll bust you out of there... just as soon as I get a cocktail dress and a crowbar.
jokes.setup: Bart promising to break Marge out of prison
jokes.punchline: Bizarre combination of cross-dressing and tools for the jailbreak
jokes.explanation: Great absurd combination of elements for escape plan, very Bart

jokes.text: And it's not just the sex. It's also the food preparation. Your skill with stains of all kinds. But mostly, I'll miss how lucky you make me feel each and every morning.
jokes.setup: Homer telling Marge what he'll miss about her
jokes.punchline: Starting crude, getting domestic, ending genuinely sweet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character arc from crude to touching in one speech

jokes.text: I'm Phillips. They call me that 'cause I killed my husband with a Phillips head screwdriver.
jokes.setup: Marge meeting her cellmate
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation of murder weapon nickname
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor delivered casually, efficient character introduction

jokes.text: Homer, I don't think this fish is quite dead yet. Marge, please, I'm having enough trouble with the lobsters.
jokes.setup: Flashback to Homer's one attempt at cooking
jokes.punchline: Fish still alive, lobsters presumably causing even more chaos
jokes.explanation: Good escalation implying kitchen chaos, typical Homer incompetence

jokes.text: Amazing!
jokes.setup: Marge seeing Mad Magazine fold-in for first time
jokes.punchline: Her innocent amazement at simple magazine trick
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's sheltered nature

jokes.text: Who's gonna change Maggie? We're gonna let her roam free in the backyard... and nature will take its course.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about household responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Homer's plan to let baby live like wild animal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer laziness taken to absurd extreme with baby care

jokes.text: It seems like I've been wearing this same red dress forever.
jokes.setup: Family running out of clean clothes
jokes.punchline: Lisa commenting on her perpetual outfit choice
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about cartoon character clothing consistency

jokes.text: A pack of sugar and peanut butter smeared on a playing card.
jokes.setup: Kids comparing lunches during Homer's domestic management
jokes.punchline: Bart's completely inappropriate 'sandwich' substitute
jokes.explanation: Good absurd meal showing household deterioration under Homer

jokes.text: We flushed the gator down the toilet, but it got stuck halfway. Now we have to feed it.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how things are at home
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of toilet alligator as ongoing household issue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd domestic situation delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Oh, come on! He's history's greatest monster!
jokes.setup: Town getting Jimmy Carter statue instead of Lincoln
jokes.punchline: Crowd's extreme overreaction to mild former president
jokes.explanation: Iconic overreaction to innocuous president, became cultural reference

jokes.text: Yeah, I've been starvin' 'em, teasin' 'em, singing off-key.
jokes.setup: Guard explaining why the dogs look angry for crowd control
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of abuse ending with singing off-key
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from physical to psychological to musical torture

jokes.text: He says his name is Homer, and he works at the nuclear plant.
jokes.setup: Phillips describing her new boyfriend to Marge
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly describing Marge's husband Homer
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony setup for awkward situation

jokes.text: We'll just sweep it all under the rug.
jokes.setup: Kids trying to clean house before Marge returns
jokes.punchline: Literally sweeping everything under rug, causing house to bulge/collapse
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag taking common expression literally with consequences

jokes.text: From now on, I'll use my gossip for good instead of evil.
jokes.setup: Helen apologizing for spreading rumors about Marge
jokes.punchline: Treating gossip like superpower with moral alignment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment framing Helen's gossip in superhero terms
